covered_sentences: Ah, Nelly, he said when they were gone, what a ridiculous end to all my efforts.
I have spent my life trying to destroy two families.
Now that everything is mine, I find I no longer want to punish them.
My old enemies have not beaten me.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Intimate two-shot in an emptied room: Heathcliff stands near the fireplace at right, not facing it but turned at a three-quarter angle toward Nelly who sits slightly below his eyeline at left-center; the vastness of the stone room around them emphasizes how much space the two departed young figures have left behind; the composition is off-balance — Heathcliff occupies more frame weight but seems diminished by the empty chairs.

Heathcliff (C1) speaks with an expression of hollow irony rather than triumph — his body language is uncharacteristically still, one hand resting loosely on the mantelpiece without gripping it, shoulders carrying a subtle downward cast that contradicts his words of victory; he looks not at Nelly but slightly past her or into the middle distance. Nelly (C7) sits upright with sewing or hands folded in her lap, watching him with careful attentiveness — her posture shows she is listening with full caution, absorbing the strangeness of his mood.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): emptied of the young people — the chairs where Catherine and Hareton sat are visible but vacant, the book left on the table; stone walls, wooden beams, the large fireplace at right with a low steady fire; the room feels larger and quieter than before.

Firelight from the right is now the only significant light source — it catches the right side of Heathcliff's face and the mantelpiece edge, leaving his left side in deep shadow; the warm amber glow does not comfort here but instead isolates him against the cold grey stone; Nelly is lit more evenly by reflected firelight, her face readable and calm; the overall palette is dim amber and deep shadow, with grey-blue stone in background.

Heathcliff (C1): tall adult male around 40, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, sharp black eyes now carrying an unfamiliar vacancy, dark gentleman's coat, white shirt, dark trousers, leather boots — his height still imposing but his energy subdued. Nelly Dean (C7): middle-aged woman around 35-45, dark brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face showing careful attentiveness, dark housekeeper dress with white apron, seated position.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within the image; no modern objects; Heathcliff's emotional register must be precisely calibrated — not broken, not triumphant, but hollowed-out and bewildered by his own indifference; the empty chairs must read as significant absence; avoid depicting Heathcliff as sympathetic in an obvious way — the ambiguity is essential.

covered_sentences: Now I could destroy their offspring completely, but what's the use?
I no longer want to give them pain.
It is not that I have decided to be generous to them.
It's simply that I no longer enjoy tormenting them.
Hareton reminds me of myself at his age.
And he also looks like Catherine, my Catherine.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Single sustained shot: Heathcliff has moved from the mantelpiece and now stands or sits in a chair facing the fire, his profile to camera, speaking as if thinking aloud; Nelly remains visible in the near background, a still witness; the fire itself occupies significant compositional space at right — Heathcliff's gaze is directed into the flames.

Heathcliff (C1) is turned toward the fire, one elbow resting on a chair arm or knee, chin slightly lowered — the posture of a man reasoning with himself rather than addressing a listener; his expression is introspective, showing not grief but a strange emotional numbness — the absence of appetite for cruelty rather than the presence of remorse. Nelly (C7) sits quietly in the near background, her needlework set down, watching his profile with an expression of subdued astonishment.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): fireplace large and prominent at right with a brighter fire than the previous beat — the flames are the visual anchor; stone walls and wooden beams in background; the vacant chairs of the young pair remain visible at left edge; the room's darkness presses in at the periphery.

The fire is now the dominant light and emotional symbol of this beat — its flickering warm light plays across Heathcliff's profile, alternately illuminating and shadowing his face with each imagined flicker; the warmth is isolating rather than comforting, like a man staring into something he cannot name; deep shadows fill the room behind him; Nelly is in softer secondary firelight; the palette moves toward deep amber, ember-orange, and near-black shadow.

Heathcliff (C1): tall adult male around 40, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, sharp black eyes reflecting firelight — in this moment those eyes are distant and unguarded, dark gentleman's coat, seated or standing in profile. Nelly Dean (C7): middle-aged woman, dark pinned hair, round kind face now showing quiet astonishment, dark housekeeper dress with white apron, seated in the background.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within image; no modern objects; the fire must read as psychologically charged — it is what Heathcliff stares into as he speaks of the people who remind him of Catherine; avoid making Heathcliff appear regretful or tender — the tone is exhaustion of appetite, not redemption; Nelly's witnessing presence must remain legible without competing with the central figure.

covered_sentences: But I don't need Hareton to remind me of her.
I think about her all the time.
I see her face in every cloud, in every tree.
The whole world is a reminder that she existed and I lost her.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Close-up psychological portrait: tight on Heathcliff's face turned slightly upward as if addressing the ceiling or the air above him — his gaze unfocused, directed nowhere in the room; Nelly is visible only as a small figure in the extreme lower corner of the frame; the stone wall and the dark upper space of the room dominate the background, vast and indifferent.

Heathcliff (C1) stands or sits with head tilted faintly back, eyes open but not seeing the room — his expression carries the weight of chronic, unassuaged loss that has become his entire interiority; his lips may be barely parted as he speaks the last words; his hands are still, offering no gesture — all expression is in the face and the quality of the gaze directed inward. Nelly (C7) is reduced to a small watchful presence in the lower frame corner, her face upturned toward him, silent.

Wuthering Heights parlor (S1): the stone ceiling and upper beams fill the upper half of the frame, dark and weathered; the firelight from off-frame right barely reaches this upper zone, leaving cold grey stone dominant; the room's height is exploited to create a sense of the world pressing down on a lone figure.

The light is sparse and dramatic — a single narrow band of firelight catches Heathcliff's jaw and cheekbone from below and right, leaving his brow and upper face in cool stone-reflected shadow; the darkness above is absolute and textured by stone; no warm comfort in the palette here — deep charcoal, cold grey, narrow ember-gold on skin; the mood is desolate and interior.

Heathcliff (C1): tall adult male around 40, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, sharp black eyes now directed inward — the sharpness of those eyes has turned to a haunted vacancy, dark gentleman's coat, white shirt visible at the collar; the top of his head near the upper stone of the room emphasizes his height. Nelly Dean (C7): small in lower corner, dark pinned hair and white apron barely lit, face tilted upward toward him.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within image; no depictions of Catherine's face as a ghost or vision — the haunting must be entirely in Heathcliff's expression and the compositional emptiness around him; no modern elements; the emotional register is absolute loss rendered as numbness, not melodrama; the stone environment must feel indifferent to human grief.

covered_sentences: A few weeks after that, Heathcliff was away from home all night.
The next morning Catherine went out to pick some flowers.
When she came back she said she had seen Heathcliff and that he looked happy.
I waited for him anxiously, curious to see what he looked like when he was happy.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Two-beat composition across open moorland: PANEL A — wide landscape shot of Catherine alone on the moorland path in early morning light, small figure amid vast heather and rolling hills, a distant solitary figure of Heathcliff barely visible on the hillside behind her; PANEL B — Catherine returning to the farmhouse gate or doorstep, her face turned back over her shoulder toward the moor, expression carrying wonder and confusion, a small clutch of wild flowers in her hand.

PANEL A: Catherine (C6b) walks along a winding sheep path through heather, her petite frame dwarfed by the open moorland; she has not yet noticed the distant figure — Heathcliff (C1) appears as a dark silhouette on a far hillside, standing still, his posture for once relaxed rather than rigid — the first visual suggestion of something changed in him. PANEL B: Catherine stands at the threshold of return, flowers loosely held, head turned back to look at the moor — her expression is one of genuine puzzlement mixed with tentative relief, as if she saw something she cannot fully explain.

Open moorland (S9): foreground of heather and moss in early morning light, the winding sheep path running mid-ground toward rolling hills in the background; a weather-beaten dwarf tree leans right in the mid-ground; the sky is large and pale — cloud formations that in the previous interior beat Heathcliff spoke of as holding Catherine's face; no farmhouse architecture in PANEL A, but PANEL B may show the edge of a stone wall or gate.

Early morning light — cool blue-white sky with the first warm yellow touching the horizon line; the heather catches the low angled light in pale purple-grey and ochre tones; Heathcliff's distant silhouette is lit from behind by the horizon light, making him appear almost luminous in contrast to his usual shadowed interiority; Catherine's figure in PANEL A receives the cool diffused morning light evenly; PANEL B shifts slightly warmer as she moves toward shelter; the palette is expansive and airy — a deliberate contrast to the stone-enclosed darkness of S1.

Catherine (C6b): young woman around 17-18, golden curly hair slightly disheveled by the moor wind, dark eyes, fair complexion, petite slender frame in plain dark dress past the knee — in PANEL A she is a small moving figure in a vast landscape; in PANEL B her face is visible with the puzzled wondering expression and wild flowers held loosely. Heathcliff (C1): visible only as a distant dark silhouette on the hillside in PANEL A — tall, still, his posture subtly different from usual — not predatory or tense but simply present in the landscape.

STYLE_PLACEHOLDER

No text within image; no modern elements; the moor must feel genuinely vast — Catherine's smallness in PANEL A is compositionally essential; Heathcliff in PANEL A must be distant enough to be ambiguous but recognizable by silhouette; do not depict his face or expression in this panel — the mystery of his happiness must remain at a distance; wild moorland flowers in Catherine's hand should be botanically plausible for the setting.
